name: King David
role: King of Israel (~1000–961 BCE); founder of the Davidic dynasty and Jerusalem as capital
quote: I will give you rest from all your enemies. The Lord declares to you that the Lord himself will establish a house for you. [2 Samuel 7:11 — Divine covenant with David, c. 1000 BCE]

name: King Solomon
role: King of Israel (~961–930 BCE); builder of the First Temple and legendary sage
quote: I have indeed built a magnificent temple for you, a place for you to dwell forever. [1 Kings 8:13 — Solomon's prayer at the Temple dedication, c. 957 BCE]

name: Moses
role: Prophet and lawgiver; traditional author of the Torah, leader of the Exodus (c. 13th century BCE)
quote: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of Egypt, out of the land of slavery. You shall have no other gods before me. [Exodus 20:2–3 — The Ten Commandments, Sinai]

name: Judas Maccabaeus
role: Military leader of the Maccabean revolt against the Seleucid Empire (~168–160 BCE); restored Temple worship
quote: It is better for us to die in battle than to witness the calamities of our people and the sanctuary. As the will of God in heaven may be, so may He do. [1 Maccabees 3:59–60, c. 165 BCE]

name: Herod the Great
role: Roman client King of Judea (37–4 BCE); builder of the expanded Temple, Caesarea Maritima, and Masada
quote: He built the sanctuary itself was built of hard, white stones, each of which was about 25 cubits in length, 8 in height, and 12 in width. [Josephus, Jewish War V.5 — describing Herod's Temple, c. 20 BCE]

name: Simon Bar Kokhba
role: Leader of the Third Jewish–Roman War (Bar Kokhba revolt, 132–135 CE); proclaimed messiah by Rabbi Akiva
quote: Simon Bar Kosiba to Yeshua son of Galgula: I call heaven to witness against me that if any of the Galileans who are with you is harmed I shall put fetters on your feet as I did to Ben Aphlu. [Bar Kokhba letter, Cave of Letters, c. 134 CE]

name: Cyrus the Great
role: Founder of the Achaemenid Persian Empire (559–530 BCE); issued the Edict allowing Jews to return from Babylon
quote: The Lord, the God of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth and he has appointed me to build a temple for him at Jerusalem in Judah. [Ezra 1:2 — Cyrus's Edict, 539 BCE]

name: Nebuchadnezzar II
role: King of Babylon (605–562 BCE); destroyed Jerusalem and the First Temple (586 BCE) and exiled the Judeans
quote: In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty and two persons. [Jeremiah 52:29 — Babylonian deportation record, 586 BCE]

name: Alexander the Great
role: King of Macedon and conqueror (356–323 BCE); took Judea in 332 BCE and inaugurated the Hellenistic period
quote: When he had overthrown Darius, he sent to the high priest to send him aid and supply his army with provisions, and give him presents. [Josephus, Antiquities XI.8 — Alexander approaches Jerusalem, 332 BCE]

name: Ezra the Scribe
role: Jewish priest and scribe; led the second wave of return from Babylon (~458 BCE); codified the Torah
quote: Ezra had devoted himself to the study and observance of the Law of the Lord, and to teaching its decrees and laws in Israel. [Ezra 7:10 — description of Ezra's mission, c. 458 BCE]

name: Titus Flavius Vespasianus
role: Roman general (later Emperor); commanded the siege of Jerusalem in 70 CE and destroyed the Second Temple
quote: This was no work of mine. God has shown his wrath, and used me only as his instrument in punishing this people. [Josephus, Jewish War VI.9 — attributed to Titus at the Temple's destruction, 70 CE]

name: Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab
role: Second Caliph of Islam (634–644 CE); personally received Jerusalem's surrender and protected its holy sites
quote: In the name of God, the merciful, the compassionate. This is the assurance of safety which Umar, the servant of God, the commander of the faithful, grants to the people of Jerusalem. Their lives and properties are safe. [Covenant of Umar, 638 CE]

name: Saladin (Salah al-Din Yusuf ibn Ayyub)
role: Sultan of Egypt and Syria, founder of Ayyubid dynasty; reconquered Jerusalem from Crusaders in 1187 CE
quote: Jerusalem is ours as much as it is yours. It was the site of our Prophet's Night Journey and we shall not give it up. [attributed to Saladin in negotiations with Richard I, 1191 CE]

name: Suleiman the Magnificent
role: Ottoman Sultan (1520–1566); rebuilt Jerusalem's city walls and renovated the Dome of the Rock
quote: Fortify the walls of Jerusalem and furnish water to its inhabitants, for the holy city is in the care of the Commander of the Faithful. [Attributed firman ordering Jerusalem's wall construction, 1537]

name: Theodor Herzl
role: Viennese journalist and founder of modern Zionism; convened the First Zionist Congress (1897)
quote: If you will it, it is no dream. [Altneuland (The Old New Land), 1902 — the founding motto of the Zionist movement]

name: David Ben-Gurion
role: First Prime Minister and founding father of the State of Israel; read the Declaration of Independence (1948)
quote: The State of Israel will be open for Jewish immigration and for the Ingathering of the Exiles. [Israeli Declaration of Independence, 14 May 1948]

name: Golda Meir
role: Fourth Prime Minister of Israel (1969–1974); led Israel through the Yom Kippur War
quote: We can forgive the Arabs for killing our children. We cannot forgive them for forcing us to kill their children. We will only have peace with the Arabs when they love their children more than they hate us. [Golda Meir, widely attributed, c. 1972]

name: Yitzhak Rabin
role: Fifth and Eleventh Prime Minister of Israel; signed the Oslo Accords (1993); assassinated November 1995
quote: We who have fought against you, the Palestinians — we say to you today in a loud and a clear voice: Enough of blood and tears. Enough. [Oslo Accords ceremony, White House, September 13, 1993]

name: Yasser Arafat
role: Chairman of the PLO (1969–2004) and President of the Palestinian Authority; co-signed the Oslo Accords
quote: I have come bearing an olive branch and a freedom-fighter's gun. Do not let the olive branch fall from my hand. [Yasser Arafat, UN General Assembly address, November 13, 1974]

name: Mahmoud Abbas (Abu Mazen)
role: President of the Palestinian Authority (2005–present); Chair of the PLO; co-architect of Oslo Accords
quote: We want to live in peace and security, just like all the other peoples of this world. I have come to you with a message of peace from the Palestinian people. [Mahmoud Abbas, UN General Assembly, September 2011]
